metadata.description: Comprehensive storage technology database aligned with the Open Energy Ontology (OEO). Efficiency values represent round-trip efficiency (RTE) for electrical storage, or charge/discharge efficiency for thermal/chemical storage. Designed for PyPSA, Calliope, OSeMOSYS, and ADOPTNet0.
metadata.notes: For storage: typical_capacity_mw = rated power output; efficiency_percent = round-trip efficiency (electrical) or thermal/chemical storage efficiency; ramping_rate_percent_per_min = discharge ramp rate.
primary_sources: NREL Grid Energy Storage Technology Cost and Performance Assessment 2022
Lazard LCOE and LCOS Analysis v8.0 (2023)
PNNL 2022 Grid Energy Storage Cost and Performance Review

technologies.description: Battery energy storage systems using lithium-ion chemistry (NMC, LFP, NCA). LFP (lithium iron phosphate) dominates grid-scale applications due to safety and cycle life. Includes inverter, BMS, HVAC, and balance-of-plant.

instances.instance_name: Li-ion BESS – 10 MW / 20 MWh LFP (2-hour, Current 2024)
Li-ion BESS – 100 MW / 400 MWh LFP (4-hour, Current 2024)
Li-ion BESS – 200 MW / 800 MWh LFP Advanced (Future, 2035)
instances.reference_source: NREL ATB 2023 – Utility-scale Battery Storage, Advanced 2035
IRENA island grid 2023; Wärtsilä Greensmith island

instances.instance_name: Li-ion BESS – 500 MWh Offshore Wind Buffer (2025)

instances.reference_source: CATL EnerC container 2023; Tesla Megapack modular

technologies.description: Electrochemical storage systems where energy is stored in liquid electrolytes circulated through a cell stack. Vanadium redox flow batteries (VRFB) are the dominant commercial variant. Power and energy are independently scalable. Well-suited for 4–12 hour storage.

instances.reference_source: IRENA Electricity Storage Roadmap 2020 – Future Projections
Sumitomo Electric VRB 2023; Rongke Power China

instances.instance_name: Redox Flow – 50 MWh Solar+VRB Cement Plant (2025)

technologies.description: High-temperature (300–350 °C) sodium-sulfur (NaS) batteries used for daily load-leveling and peak-shaving. Typically 6–8 hour discharge duration. Primarily manufactured by NGK Insulators (Japan). Long cycle life but requires thermal management.

instances.reference_source: NGK INSULATORS NaS Battery Data Sheet 2022

instances.instance_name: NaS Battery – 108 MWh ADWEA Abu Dhabi (2023)
NaS Battery – 34 MWh Wind Integration Japan (2024)
NaS Battery – 15 MWh Island Grid Alaska (2024)
NaS Battery – 7.2 MWh Substation T&D Support (2024)
NaS Battery – 100 MWh Solar Co-located Morocco (2025)
NaS Battery – 20 MWh South Africa Grid (2024)
instances.reference_source: Eskom SA 2022; NGK South Africa deployment

technologies.description: Mature electrochemical energy storage using lead electrodes and sulfuric acid electrolyte. Advanced VRLA (valve-regulated) and flooded variants used for telecom backup, UPS, and small-grid applications. Low cost but limited cycle life and lower energy density than lithium-ion.

instances.instance_name: Lead-Acid – 1 MW / 4 MWh Flooded (Island Grid Storage, 4h)
Lead-Acid – 5 MW / 20 MWh Advanced Carbon (AGM, Enhanced Cycle Life)

instances.reference_source: IEEE TEC lead-acid; East Penn flooded
Exide/GS Yuasa 2022; Sandia natl lab lead
US DoD field storage 2022; EnerSys Odyssey

technologies.description: Pumps water from a lower to an upper reservoir during low-cost electricity periods and generates electricity by reversing flow through turbines. The dominant form of bulk, long-duration grid storage globally. Variable speed (adjustable speed) units provide additional grid services.

instances.instance_name: Pumped Hydro – 1000 MW / 10000 MWh (10-hour, Large Plant)
Pumped Hydro – 500 MW Adjustable Speed (Variable Speed, Ternary Set)

instances.reference_source: IRENA PHS 2023; IEA World Energy Outlook
ARES 2023; US DOE mine pumped hydro
Frades II 2015; IEA PHS variable speed

instances.instance_name: Pumped Hydro – 200 MW Existing Dam Retrofit (2024)
Pumped Hydro – 300 MW Grid Stabilization UK (2024)
Pumped Hydro – 1200 MW Three Gorges Style (2024)
instances.reference_source: China NEA PHS 2023; IHA Three Gorges
ANU GIS 2023; US DOE closed-loop PHS
Tidal Lagoon Power 2022; Swansea Bay study

technologies.description: Stores energy by compressing air into underground caverns or pressure vessels. During discharge, compressed air is expanded through turbines. Diabatic CAES requires supplemental gas heating. Advanced adiabatic CAES (A-CAES) stores compression heat to eliminate fuel use.

instances.instance_name: CAES – 100 MW Diabatic (Underground Cavern, e.g., Huntorf-class)
CAES – 300 MW Large Diabatic (Salt Cavern, Next-Gen)
CAES – 200 MW Advanced Adiabatic A-CAES (Gas-free, Future 2035)
instances.reference_source: ALACAES A-CAES Pilot Study 2022; SCCER STORAGE Report 2023
Iowa Stored Energy Park 2022; EPRI aquifer
US DOE mine storage 2023; Sandia CAES

instances.instance_name: CAES – 400 MW Advanced Grid CAES 2030 Forecast
CAES – 150 MW Hard Rock Cavern Norway (2027)

technologies.description: Cryogenic energy storage system that liquefies air (−196 °C) using off-peak electricity and re-gasifies it to drive a turbine on discharge. Also known as Cryogenic Energy Storage (CES). Highview Power is the primary commercial developer.

instances.instance_name: LAES – 5 MW / 15 MWh Pilot Plant (Highview Power, 2024)
LAES – 50 MW / 250 MWh Commercial Scale (Highview CRYOBattery)
LAES – 50 MW with Industrial Waste Heat (2026)
instances.reference_source: NRRI LAES waste heat 2022; Linde Air
IRENA LAES island 2022; ETH Zurich LAES
Tokyo Gas cold recovery 2022; Air Products

instances.instance_name: LAES – 300 MW North Sea Offshore Hub (2030)

technologies.description: Kinetic energy storage using a rotating mass (rotor) in a vacuum enclosure with magnetic bearings. Extremely high power-to-energy ratio, fast response (millisecond), very high cycle life. Used for frequency regulation, UPS, and short-duration grid services.

instances.instance_name: Flywheel – 0.25 MW / 0.06 MWh Steel Rotor (Frequency Regulation, 15 min)
Flywheel – 20 MW Regulation Plant (200 × 100 kW Units, Composite Rotor)

instances.reference_source: IRENA island flywheel 2022; El Hierro Gorona

technologies.description: Thermal energy storage using the heat capacity of a medium (water, molten salt, rock, concrete, oil) without phase change. Includes hot water tanks, molten-salt two-tank systems (CSP integration), and pit thermal energy storage (PTES) for district heating.

instances.instance_name: Sensible TES – 5 MWh Pressurized Hot Water Tank (District Heating)
Sensible TES – 100 MWh Molten Salt Two-Tank (CSP Integration, 6h)
Sensible TES – 1000 MWh Pit Thermal Energy Storage PTES (Seasonal District Heating)
Sensible TES – 200 m³ Hot Water Tank Residential (2024)

instances.reference_source: Marstal Fjernvarme pit TES 2022; SDH IEA
Drake Landing BTES 2022; IEA solar heat

instances.instance_name: Sensible TES – Two-Tank Molten Salt CSP 15 h (2024)

Sensible TES – 300,000 m³ Seasonal Solar DH (2025)
Sensible TES – 5 MWh DC Free-Cooling Tank (2024)
Sensible TES – 50 MWh Cement Kiln Integration (2026)
Sensible TES – 200 MWh Nuclear Steam Buffer (2026)
instances.reference_source: INL nuclear TES 2023; NuScale molten salt

technologies.description: Thermal energy storage exploiting the latent heat of phase-change materials (PCMs) at constant temperature. High energy density. Applications include building thermal management, industrial process heat, and solar thermal systems. Materials range from paraffin waxes to salt hydrates.

instances.instance_name: Latent TES – 0.1 MWh PCM Building HVAC (Paraffin, ~25 °C)
Latent TES – 5 MWh PCM Industrial Process Heat (Erythritol, ~120 °C)
Latent TES – 50 MWh PCM Utility CSP Integration (KNO3/NaNO3 Salt, 250 °C)
Latent TES – 1 MWh Ice Storage Commercial (2024)

instances.reference_source: Fraunhofer ISE fatty acid 2022; IEA solar

NREL erythritol PCM 2022; IEA renewable heat

technologies.description: Above-ground compressed hydrogen storage at high pressure (350–700 bar) in steel or composite Type IV cylinders/vessels. Used in hydrogen refuelling stations, industrial H2 buffer storage, and energy storage systems. efficiency_percent represents round-trip compressor/decompressor efficiency.

H2 Tanks – 700 bar Composite Type IV (Hydrogen Refuelling Station)
H2 Tanks – Liquid H2 Cryogenic Storage (LH2, Large-Scale Export Terminal)

technologies.description: Large-scale, long-duration hydrogen storage in geological formations: salt caverns (most mature), depleted gas fields, and aquifers. Enables seasonal balancing of hydrogen supply and demand at TWh scale. efficiency_percent covers compressor energy costs.

instances.instance_name: H2 Underground – Salt Cavern ~100 GWh H2 (Small, Single Cavern)
